data.narrative:
Gauss Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised €9M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy: three public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable here. While eight same-sector nuclear energy peers exist, the filtering constraints—same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—yield insufficient datapoints (fewer than three comparables meeting any single criterion). Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias and misrepresent the nuclear subsector's unique capital intensity and development timelines. No other quantitative methods produced valid outputs with the available dataset.

The absence of a defensible comparable-company multiple or precedent transaction analysis leaves this valuation unconstrained by the triangulation framework. Key uncertainties include the scarcity of Series A nuclear energy companies with disclosed terms, the wide heterogeneity in technology readiness across the peer set (ranging from pre-revenue to advanced stages), and limited public market depth for pure-play nuclear development. A valuation range cannot be supported without either (a) expanding the peer set to include adjacent energy or deep-tech infrastructure stages, or (b) deploying discounted cash flow analysis conditional on technology-specific assumptions about regulatory timelines and deployment scenarios.
